name: NexusFlow
description: Architected a production-grade inventory and order management platform implementing transactional order processing, stock validation, and ACID-compliant business workflows.
Designed scalable system architecture using cache-aside patterns, automatic cache invalidation, repository-service design, and Docker-based deployments.

name: BeyondEvent
description: Designed and built an event-driven architecture simulation platform where developers model distributed systems in an interactive graph editor, execute simulations over the topology, and watch events propagate live on the canvas with per-service worker lifecycle states.

Architected a transport-agnostic event bus (Redis Streams and in-memory adapters behind one interface) with idempotent event persistence, distributed tracing via trace/span/correlation/causation IDs, and deterministic replay that re-delivers recorded events without duplication.

Built a reactive worker runtime: a Worker SDK with retries, timeouts, and lifecycle heartbeats for external processes, plus a bus-driven executor that activates user-defined workers by event-type subscription — keeping workers fully decoupled from topology definitions.

Implemented chaos engineering (fault injection, latency, event duplication) with a timeline scrubber for post-run debugging, real-time Socket.IO streaming to a React 19 dashboard, and CI-run integration tests that boot the full Fastify API against real Postgres and Redis containers.
